prevalence.trend: increasing due to obesity and population aging

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; elevated via cardiovascular and renal complications

vulnerablePopulations: indigenous and rural populations with limited access
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Fasting plasma glucose ≥126 mg/dL, HbA1c ≥6.5%, 2h OGTT ≥200 mg/dL, or random glucose ≥200 mg/dL with symptoms; per SBD/ADA-aligned criteria

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: opportunistic screening in APS; variable by municipality
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: ~45-55% reach glycemic targets; constrained by follow-up capacity and adherence

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS screening with fasting glucose/HbA1c where available; complication screening (retina, kidney, feet) uneven
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle + metformin first-line; add sulfonylurea/insulin via SUS; GLP-1RA/SGLT2i limited in SUS and more common in private care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Obesity epidemic, delayed complication screening, and unequal access to newer cardioprotective agents in SUS
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Broad SUS availability of metformin/sulfonylureas/insulin and strong APS platform for chronic care
